mnemonics: PLANE: Protocol Layering And Network Elements - A mnemonic to remember the layered architecture and key components.
keyPoints: The layered protocol architecture (like TCP/IP) simplifies design and interoperability.
The end-to-end principle argues that core network features should be kept simple, with intelligence at the endpoints.
Performance metrics like bandwidth, latency, and throughput are fundamental to understanding network behavior.
commonMistakes: Confusing routing with switching (routing finds the path, switching forwards the data).
Assuming a reliable link layer eliminates the need for transport layer reliability (like TCP).
Equating bandwidth with speed; bandwidth is capacity (width of the pipe), while latency is delay (time for data to travel).
